coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like digital gold. It does not earn fees — its value comes from people trusting it as a safe place to store wealth, the same way people have trusted gold for centuries. We use two checks: (1) How much of gold's total value has this asset captured so far? If that share grows, the price goes up. (2) How much does it cost to produce one coin? Prices rarely stay below production cost for long because miners would stop mining, reducing supply. The Speculative Fair Value is the average of both checks.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.0/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-26.9%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 33.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket B (Store of Value): Up to 6 methods are available — Monetary Premium, Production Cost Floor, Metcalfe Network Value, Supply Adjusted Value, Network Activity (NVT), and Rank Benchmark. Monetary Premium measures the token's current share of gold's market cap, projecting fair value if that share is maintained as gold appreciates. Production Cost Floor is unavailable: this token is not a PoW-mineable asset or mining cost data is not available. Supply Adjusted Value penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — inconsistent with a credible scarcity claim. Network Activity (NVT) treats volume as monetary circulation velocity — higher NVT targets apply to SoV assets held long-term rather than traded for fees. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

metric_assessments.scarcity: Zcash scores 2 out of 10 on scarcity, which measures how limited the supply of coins is—a key factor in whether a currency can hold value over time, similar to how gold's rarity supports its price. The data shows a 20.53% supply overhang, meaning there are significantly more coins waiting to be released into circulation than currently exist. This large pipeline of future supply dilution undermines Zcash's ability to function as a reliable store of value, as the asset will become substantially less scarce in the coming years.
metric_assessments.adoption_momentum: Zcash scores 5 out of 10 on adoption momentum, which tracks whether more people and institutions are actually using and accepting the asset—essential for any cryptocurrency claiming to be a store of value. With a rank proxy value of 0.0527, Zcash sits at #13 by market cap but shows limited real-world usage momentum compared to its market position. For a beginner investor, this means Zcash may be valued more on speculation than on genuine, growing demand from users.
metric_assessments.security_premium: Zcash scores 5 out of 10 on security premium, which measures the price investors are willing to pay relative to the cost of producing new coins—indicating confidence in the asset's longevity and trustworthiness. The data provided no specific price-to-production-cost ratio to evaluate, limiting our assessment. Without this crucial data point, it's difficult to determine if Zcash's current $664.04 price reflects genuine security and scarcity or is primarily driven by speculation.
metric_assessments.monetary_premium_quality: Zcash scores 2 out of 10 on monetary premium quality, which compares the total future potential value (fully diluted) against today's market value—a measure of how much inflation risk exists. The FDV-to-market-cap ratio of 1.26 indicates that the fully diluted value is only 26% higher than the current market cap, which sounds positive, but combined with the 20.53% supply overhang, it reveals significant near-term dilution ahead. This score suggests that Zcash's monetary properties are weak relative to what a true store of value should offer.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Zcash scores 6 out of 10 on dilution control, which measures what percentage of the total future supply is already in circulation—higher percentages mean less room for dilution. Currently, 79.47% of Zcash's eventual total supply is already circulating, leaving about 20% still to be released. While this is better than assets with 99%+ circulating percentages, it still represents meaningful future dilution that will pressure the price and weaken its store-of-value credentials.
metric_assessments.market_resilience: Zcash scores 10 out of 10 on market resilience, which measures price stability and recovery strength over short timeframes—showing whether the asset can weather market volatility. The blended 30-day and 7-day price momentum of 75.6% (with a 30-day gain of 113.85%) indicates Zcash has been extremely volatile and trending upward recently. For a store of value, this extreme volatility is actually a warning sign: genuine stores of value should be stable anchors, not speculative assets experiencing triple-digit monthly swings.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Zcash receives a 'Dangerous' rating because its fundamental store-of-value properties are severely compromised. Despite ranking #13 by market cap, it scores only 2 out of 10 on scarcity (with 20.53% supply overhang waiting to dilute holders), 2 out of 10 on monetary premium quality, and shows weak adoption momentum relative to its market position. The recent 113.85% price surge reflects speculation rather than sound fundamentals, making this a high-risk asset for investors seeking stability.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Zcash is a privacy-focused blockchain that positions itself as digital cash, but it fundamentally fails to deliver the scarcity and stability required of a true store of value. With nearly one-fifth of its eventual supply still unissued and a fully diluted market cap only 26% larger than today's value, significant dilution pressure awaits existing holders—directly undermining the core promise of value preservation. While the recent 113% price surge shows market interest, this reflects speculative trading rather than growing adoption or monetary strength, as evidenced by weak adoption momentum scores. For a beginner investor seeking a store of value to preserve wealth, Zcash presents unacceptable dilution risk and volatility; the $664 price appears driven by speculation rather than durable fundamentals, making this a bet on future sentiment rather than a reliable wealth anchor.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Zcash scores 5.0/10 (Dangerous) as a Store of Value. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.
